title: Gymnadenia albida
intro: Gymnadenia albida, also known as the white fragrant orchid or white lady's-tresses, is a perennial orchid native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Gymnadenia albida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnadenia albida is often prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in the form of infusions or decoctions to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Gymnadenia albida is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Orchideae. Additionally, it is placed in the genus Gymnadenia. Gymnadenia albida is further classified as a species within the Orchidaceae family.
uses: Gymnadenia albida, also known as the narrow-leaved fragrant orchid, has been traditionally used in medicine. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Gymnadenia albida is believed to have applications in treating various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Gymnadenia albida in treating these conditions. However, its traditional uses highlight the plant's potential as a natural remedy.

preparations: Gymnadenia albida is a plant species used in traditional herbal medicine, where preparations are made from its roots and flowers to treat various health conditions. These preparations are believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties.
